Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Sportamore Searches. 34 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Sportamore search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Test results summary → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Sportamore's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 34 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Fix relevance score ranking across all queries — This addresses the core ranking failures affecting Product Discovery, Brand Search, Language Understanding, and Shopping Context capabilities where high-relevance products are consistently buried below lower-scoring items.

57%
revenue risk. 34 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
34
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
26
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
0
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
78%
Right products found, ranked too low
#7
Avg. best result position
67%
Best result outside top 3
19%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "löpaskor" gets zero results — a blank page, nothing at all.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 friction point.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “träningsskor” — The highest relevance result 'Nano X2 Tr Adventure Shoes Pure Grey 2' (0.703 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-relevance items appeared first.
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.

Collection routing

Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
3 probes reviewed: 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
The pattern works in places, but the experience is not consistently clean.
Example “löp” — The search engine correctly retrieved relevant running-related products for the partial query 'löp' (Swedish for running shoes), but ranked them poorly.
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.
Language Understanding
Synonym
Vocabulary mapping
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “jackor” — The search engine correctly retrieved relevant jacket products for the Swedish query 'jackor' but severely misranked them.
Does search connect customer vocabulary to the store's preferred product wording?
Next tuning move: Expand synonym dictionaries and semantic embeddings for the product language customers actually use.
Language Understanding
Natural language
Intent translation
3 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “bra skor för löpning” — The query 'bra skor för löpning' (good shoes for running) returned sports bras as top results instead of running shoes.
Can longer, conversational needs be translated into product intent?
Next tuning move: Extract intent phrases from longer queries before ranking products.

Are brand requests recognized and protected from unrelated results?
Next tuning move: Detect brand entities and boost or filter to products from the requested brand.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

32%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 32% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 28%.

Evidence

  • "löparskor": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "kläder": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "utrustning": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "pumma": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

67%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 40 of 60 queries (67%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "löpaskor": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "löparskordam": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "hoka träningsskor": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "vinter träningskläder": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

4
queries with attribute loss

Result

4 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "herr jackor vinter": dropped herr, jackor, vinter; partially preserved none
  • "nike svarta löparskor": dropped svarta, parskor; partially preserved nike
  • "dam träningskläder adidas": dropped dam, ningskl, der; partially preserved tr
  • "barn fotbollsskor nike": dropped barn, fotbollsskor; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

9
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "sneakers": 9 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "sneakers": 9 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "skor": 10 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "kläder": 10 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "utrustning": 10 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Query Specificity Scaling

This compares broad -> specific query chains to see whether search gets better as the shopper becomes clearer. High-specificity queries often signal stronger purchase intent, so degradation here is commercially important.

0
specificity ladders detected

Result

No three-step specificity ladder was detected in this query set. This diagnostic activates when generated queries include broad -> specific query chains.

How to remedy it

Generate intentional specificity ladders during query creation, then use them as regression tests. When a ladder breaks, inspect the attribute added at that step and tune query parsing or ranking weights for that attribute.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Fix relevance score ranking across all queries

This addresses the core ranking failures affecting Product Discovery, Brand Search, Language Understanding, and Shopping Context capabilities where high-relevance products are consistently buried below lower-scoring items.

Critical Quick Win
2

Add Swedish language support and compound word splitting

This fixes the zero-result failures in Typo Tolerance and Language Understanding where Swedish queries like "vandringskor," "löparskordam," and "vinter träningskläder" return nothing.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Implement Swedish abbreviation and synonym mapping

This resolves Brand & Model Search failures where "bh" returns men's underwear instead of sports bras, and improves Language Understanding for Swedish product terms.

Critical Quick Win
4

Configure multi-attribute query parsing for color, gender, and brand constraints

This fixes Filters & Constraints failures where queries like "nike svarta löparskor" ignore color requirements and "dam träningskläder" shows boys' products.

Critical Medium Effort
5

Add category filtering to prevent irrelevant product bleed

This stops rain gear from appearing in training shoe results and sports bras from dominating running shoe queries, improving Product Discovery accuracy.

Critical Quick Win
6

Implement basic typo tolerance with 1-2 character edit distance

This addresses Typo Tolerance failures where single-character mistakes like "löpaskor" and "salomon" either return zero results or poor rankings.

Critical Medium Effort
7

Add seasonal and contextual query understanding

This enables Shopping Context queries like "present till sporttjej" and "vinter träningskläder" to return appropriate product categories instead of zero results.

Critical Major Project
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 33% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #7 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 19% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 löpaskor No fuzzy matching

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

Query 'löpaskor' (missing 'r' from correct Swedish word 'löparskor' meaning running shoes) returned zero results despite the displacement metric indicating a best result exists at position #16, suggesting relevant products are in the catalog but the search engine cannot handle the single character typo

Fix

Add typo and fuzzy matching for high-value product, category, and brand terms so small spelling errors still reach the right products.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#2 löparskordam No fuzzy matching

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The query 'löparskordam' returned zero results despite being a compound Swedish word meaning 'running shoes women' (löparskor + dam). The search engine failed to recognize this as a merged compound query and did not attempt to split it into meaningful tokens or match it against existing products.

Fix

Add typo and fuzzy matching for high-value product, category, and brand terms so small spelling errors still reach the right products.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#3 hoka träningsskor Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for 'hoka träningsskor' despite this being a straightforward brand + product type query. The stats show 0 total results, max relevance score of 0.000, and the best result was displaced to position 16, indicating the engine found nothing relevant in the top positions.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#4 vinter träningskläder Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the query 'vinter träningskläder' (winter training clothes in Swedish). Despite the stats indicating a best result at position #16, the actual results shown are empty, meaning customers see no products at all when searching for winter workout clothing.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#5 rea sportkläder Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the query 'rea sportkläder' (Swedish for 'sale sportswear'), despite the analysis indicating there was a best result at position #16 with displacement of 15 positions. This suggests relevant products exist in the catalog but the search engine failed to retrieve any results at all.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

#6 snygga träningskläder Zero results

The best matching result appeared at position #16, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the Swedish query 'snygga träningskläder' (stylish training clothes), despite this being a common product category that should exist in any clothing retailer's inventory.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

Customer saw

Should have seen

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"träningsskor"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result 'Nano X2 Tr Adventure Shoes Pure Grey 2' (0.703 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-relevance items appeared first. Position #1 showed 'Nanoflex Tr 2 Barely Grey/team Tea' with only 0.621 relevance. Even worse, completely irrelevant rain outfits 'Rain outfit Tihku Greyish green' (0.301 relevance) and 'Rain outfit Tihku Yellow' (0.307 relevance) appeared at positions #5 and #6, despite having no connection to training shoes.

Customer Saw

  1. Nanoflex Tr 2 Barely Grey/team Tea (009 kr)
  2. Nano Gym Moon\lime\ White (089 kr)
  3. Nano Gym Black\digital Coral (059 kr)
  4. Response Super 3.0 Lace Shoes Core Black / Core Black / Cloud White (639 kr)
  5. Rain outfit Tihku Greyish green (519 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Nano X2 Tr Adventure Shoes Pure Grey 2 (399 kr)
  2. Response Super 3.0 Lace Shoes Core Black / Core Black / Cloud White (639 kr)
  3. Nano X2 Tr Adventure Orgfla/cblack/soacye (129 kr)

Best match was buried at position #13.

"utrustning"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result 'Gym Ball 70-75cm Black' (0.471 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-relevance items like 'Klistermärke Bokstav L' (0.387 relevance) appeared at position #2. The search engine retrieved relevant equipment items but failed to surface the most relevant ones first.

Customer Saw

  1. Ua Curry Logo Ss White (219 kr)
  2. Klistermärke Bokstav L (9 kr)
  3. Raptor Hcb Titanium Mirrored Gybkmrd (379 kr)
  4. Predator Flex Polarized Ultra Siblpcps (439 kr)
  5. Dynamic Impulse Supersoft Orange/Yellow (629 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Gym Ball 70-75cm Black (249 kr)
  2. Ua Curry Logo Ss White (219 kr)
  3. Raptor Hcb Titanium Mirrored Gybkmrd (379 kr)

Best match was buried at position #13.

"nike"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #12

The search engine correctly retrieved all Nike products but ranked them poorly. The two highest-scoring results (Nike Dri-fit Swoosh products with 0.781 relevance) were buried at positions #12 and #13, while lower-scoring items like 'Nike B 4" Volley Short Ess Black' (0.703 relevance) appeared at position #2. The best result was displaced by 11 positions from where it should have been.

Customer Saw

  1. Nike Sportswear Club Men's Jog Alligator/alligator/white (399 kr)
  2. Nike B 4" Volley Short Ess Black (299 kr)
  3. Nike Metcon 9 Men"s Training S Black/white-anthracite-smoke G (479 kr)
  4. Nike Revolution 7 Baby/toddler Football Grey/midnight Navy-li (409 kr)
  5. Nike React Hyperset Unisex Ind Black/white (299 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Nike Dri-fit Swoosh Women's Me Cinnabar/white (339 kr)
  2. Nike Dri-fit Swoosh Women’s Me Sangria/light Bordeaux/black (549 kr)
  3. Nike React Hyperset Unisex Ind Black/white (299 kr)

Best match was buried at position #12.

"kläder"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #12

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant clothing items for the Swedish query 'kläder' but severely misranked them. The highest relevance item 'Funky Tee Black' (0.609) was buried at position #12, while lower-scoring items like 'Altitude Tights Black' (0.543) and 'Cursive Bra Black' (0.523) were ranked #1 and #3 respectively. The top 3 positions contained items with relevance scores of 0.543, 0.574, and 0.523, while much better matches with scores of 0.609, 0.598, and 0.594 were relegated to positions #12, #14, and #13.

Customer Saw

  1. Altitude Tights Black (249 kr)
  2. Karmen L Puma White-puma White-puma Sil (579 kr)
  3. Cursive Bra Black (119 kr)
  4. Tonal Caviar (879 kr)
  5. Tonal Marfil (939 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Funky Tee Black (239 kr)
  2. Idur Kids Pants Sweet Pink (359 kr)
  3. Destress Tie T-shirt Pink (199 kr)

Best match was buried at position #12.

"löparskor"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #9

The best matching result 'Air Zoom Vomero 16 Men's Road Running Shoes' with relevance score 0.500 was buried at position #9, while much less relevant results like 'Floatride Energy Symmetros Black' (0.357) and 'Revolution 6 Big Kids' Road Running Shoes PEARL PINK' (0.398) occupied the top positions. The top 3 average relevance was only 0.393, well below the 0.60 threshold for good performance.

Customer Saw

  1. Floatride Energy Symmetros Black (339 kr)
  2. Revolution 6 Big Kids' Road Running Shoes PEARL PINK/COSMIC FUCHSIA-COBALT BLISS (469 kr)
  3. Pureboost 22 Shoes Core Black / Cloud White / Carbon (079 kr)
  4. Softride Vital Repel Wn's Lotus-puma White-sunblaze (439 kr)
  5. Impulse W Black/lunar Rock/black (699 kr)

Should Have Seen

  2. RunFalcon 3 Lace Shoes Cloud White / Core Black / Cloud White (299 kr)

Best match was buried at position #9.

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"löpaskor"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #16

Query 'löpaskor' (missing 'r' from correct Swedish word 'löparskor' meaning running shoes) returned zero results despite the displacement metric indicating a best result exists at position #16, suggesting relevant products are in the catalog but the search engine cannot handle the single character typo

Customer Saw

Should Have Seen

Best match was buried at position #16.

"löparskordam"
Merged words · Critical · Best result at position #16

The query 'löparskordam' returned zero results despite being a compound Swedish word meaning 'running shoes women' (löparskor + dam). The search engine failed to recognize this as a merged compound query and did not attempt to split it into meaningful tokens or match it against existing products.

Customer Saw

Should Have Seen

Best match was buried at position #16.

"under armour"
Split word · Critical · Best result at position #15

The two highest relevance Under Armour products were buried at positions #13 and #15: 'Under Armour Havoc 2' (0.777 relevance) and 'Under Armour 7/8 Tights Black' (0.785 relevance). Meanwhile, products with much lower relevance scores dominated the top positions: 'Armour Comp Sl' at #1 (0.420 relevance) and 'HG Armour Shorts Black' at #2 (0.447 relevance). The best matching result was displaced by 14 positions.

"t-shirts dam"
Special character · Critical · Best result at position #15

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant t-shirts but ranked them poorly. The two highest-scoring results 'Team II T-Shirt 4her Grey' (0.660 relevance) and 'T-shirt Exercise Women Grey' (0.641 relevance) were buried at positions #15 and #13 respectively. Meanwhile, lower-scoring results like 'Dylan LS T-Shirt Purple' (0.523) and 'Crewneck T-Shirt Black' (0.523) appeared at the top positions #1 and #2. The best result was displaced by 14 positions.

  4. Activchill Graphic Move T-Shirt Vector Blue (409 kr)
  5. Agile Ss Tee Grey (399 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Team II T-Shirt 4her Grey (319 kr)
  2. T-shirt Exercise Women Grey (339 kr)
  3. Sportamore T-shirt Pink (269 kr)

Best match was buried at position #15.

"salomon"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #11

The search engine correctly retrieved Salomon products despite the typo 'salomon' (missing 'o'), but severely misranked them. The highest relevance result 'Sense Ride GTX Noctur Black/Grey' (0.369 relevance) was buried at position #11, while lower-scoring results like 'Xa Pro V8 J Stormy Weather/zen Blue/goji B' (0.293 relevance) appeared at #1. The top 3 positions averaged only 0.293 relevance when they should have averaged 0.346.

Customer Saw

  1. Xa Pro V8 J Stormy Weather/zen Blue/goji B (499 kr)
  2. Xa Pro V8 J Black/urban Chic/sulphur (479 kr)
  3. Xa Pro 3d V8 Gtx W Black/green Moss/zen Blue (339 kr)
  4. Agile Ss Tee Grey (399 kr)
  5. Speedcross Cswp J Black/wrought Iron/lemon (659 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Sense Ride GTX Noctur Black/Grey (359 kr)
  2. Xa Pro V8 Winter Cswp J Black/phantom/quiet Shade (649 kr)
  3. Impulse W Black/lunar Rock/black (699 kr)

Best match was buried at position #11.

Language Understanding

5 examples
"jackor"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #14

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant jacket products for the Swedish query 'jackor' but severely misranked them. The highest relevance result 'K Sogn Jacket 421 Lav Green' (0.602 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-scoring results like 'Kicker Jacket Black' (0.578) and 'Jr Chloe Bomber Jacket Pink' (0.508) were ranked #1 and #4 respectively. The best result was displaced by 13 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Kicker Jacket Black (699 kr)
  2. Paulina Melange Fleece Jacket Beige (359 kr)
  3. Lingbo Shell Jacket Black (659 kr)
  4. Jr Chloe Bomber Jacket Pink (239 kr)
  5. W Frost Down Hood Jacket Black (499 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. K Sogn Jacket 421 Lav Green (939 kr)
  2. Lingbo Shell Jacket Black (659 kr)
  3. Kicker Jacket Black (699 kr)

Best match was buried at position #14.

"bra skor för löpning"
Natural language · Critical · Best result at position #11

The query 'bra skor för löpning' (good shoes for running) returned sports bras as top results instead of running shoes. Position #2 'Fe/nom Flyknit Bra Black/Grey', #3 'UA Infinity High Bra Black', and #4 'Fe/nom Flyknit Bra White/Black' are all sports bras, not shoes. The engine matched on the word 'bra' (which means 'good' in Swedish but also appears in sports bra product names) while ignoring 'skor' (shoes) and 'löpning' (running). The best running shoe 'Xa Elevate GTX®' was buried at position #11.

Customer Saw

  1. X9000L3 Shoes Core Black / Core Black / Solar Red (699 kr)
  2. Fe/nom Flyknit Bra Black/Grey (759 kr)
  3. UA Infinity High Bra Black (399 kr)
  4. Fe/nom Flyknit Bra White/Black (699 kr)
  5. Tensaur Sport Training Lace Shoes Cloud White / Cloud White / Grey One (319 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Xa Elevate GTX® Purple/Black (999 kr)
  2. Tensaur Sport Training Lace Shoes Cloud White / Cloud White / Grey One (319 kr)
  3. Metcon FlyKnit 4 White/Black (199 kr)

Best match was buried at position #11.

"vad har ni för träningskläder"
Natural language · Moderate · Best result at position #7

The search engine correctly identified relevant training clothes but ranked them poorly. The most relevant result 'Active Crop Top Black' (0.613 relevance) was buried at position #7, while less relevant items like 'Stereo Underwear Blue/White' (0.367 relevance) and 'Corinne Swimsuit Black' (0.305 relevance) appeared at positions #2 and #3. Other highly relevant training items like 'Scend Pro Puma' athletic shoes (0.504 relevance) and 'Adidas Essentials Hoodie' (0.490 relevance) were pushed down to positions #13 and #14.

Customer Saw

  1. Swoosh Women's Medium-Support 1-Piece Pad Sports Bra BLACK/WHITE (289 kr)
  2. Stereo Underwear Blue/White (79 kr)
  3. Corinne Swimsuit Black (189 kr)
  4. Crocband Navy (339 kr)
  5. Thrill Navy/Grey (459 kr)

Should Have Seen

  2. Scend Pro Puma Black-frosty Pink-speed G (599 kr)
  3. Adidas Essentials Hoodie Medium Grey Heather (399 kr)

Best match was buried at position #7.

"tröjor"
Synonym · Moderate · Best result at position #4

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant sweatshirt products for the Swedish query 'tröjor' (sweaters), but ranked them poorly. The two highest-relevance products 'Crewneck Sweatshirt Junior Red' (0.559) and 'Core Crew Sweatshirt White' (0.559) were buried at positions #4 and #15 respectively, while lower-relevance products like 'Hooded Sweatshirt Peachy Keen' (0.471) and 'Hooded Sweatshirt Black Beauty' (0.471) occupied the top two positions.

  3. Essentials Big Logo Cotton Sweatshirt Black (359 kr)

"byxor"
Synonym · Moderate · Best result at position #1

The search correctly retrieved relevant pants/trousers products for the Swedish synonym 'byxor', but the ranking is inconsistent with relevance scores. Products with higher relevance scores are ranked lower: 'Cargo Pants Khaki' (0.340 relevance) is at position #4 instead of #2, 'Inflect Tights Black' (0.338 relevance) is at position #5 instead of #3, and 'Sportswear Club Fleece Women's Mid-Rise Pants' (0.334 relevance) is buried at position #11 despite being more relevant than several products ranked above it like 'Essentials 3S Pant' (0.307 relevance) at position #3.

Filters & Constraints

4 examples
"herr jackor vinter"
Multi-attribute · Critical · Best result at position #16

The search returned zero results for the query 'herr jackor vinter' (men's jackets winter in Swedish), despite the stats indicating there was a best result at position #16 with relevance score 0.000. This suggests the engine has products that should match but failed to retrieve any results for display.

Customer Saw

Should Have Seen

Best match was buried at position #16.

"nike svarta löparskor"
Multi-attribute · Critical · Best result at position #6

The query 'nike svarta löparskor' (Nike black running shoes) should prioritize black Nike running shoes, but the top results include non-black items like 'Nike Md Valiant Baby/toddler S Mystic Navy/grey Fog-atomic Gr' (#1) and 'Nike Star Runner 3 Se Big Kids Summit White/pink Gaze -pink F' (#2). Meanwhile, the best matching black Nike running shoes 'React Miler Shield White/Black' (0.467 relevance) was buried at position #6, and other black Nike shoes like 'MD Valiant Little Kids' Shoes BLACK/WHITE' and 'MD Valiant Baby/Toddler Shoes BLACK/WHITE' were ranked #14 and #15 respectively.

  1. Nike Md Valiant Baby/toddler S Mystic Navy/grey Fog-atomic Gr (419 kr)

"dam träningskläder adidas"
Multi-attribute · Moderate · Best result at position #4

The highest relevance result 'Adidas Runner Long Sleeve Tee Women Alumina' (0.766) was ranked #4 instead of #1. Two boys' products with lower relevance scores (0.486 and 0.523) were ranked #1 and #2, despite the query 'dam träningskläder adidas' specifically requesting women's training clothes. The women's-specific result should have been prioritized.

"barn fotbollsskor nike"
Multi-attribute · Minor · Best result at position #1

The search correctly identified relevant Nike children's shoes, but the ranking algorithm prioritized some results incorrectly. For example, 'Nike Revolution 6 Little Kids'' (relevance 0.762) appeared at position #4 instead of #2, and 'Tanjun Baby/Toddler Shoes' (relevance 0.426) was buried at position #14 instead of #9. The top result was correctly positioned, but several highly relevant Nike kids' shoes were ranked below less relevant items.

Customer Saw

  1. Nike Revolution 7 Baby/toddler Football Grey/midnight Navy-li (409 kr)
  2. Nike Hydrastrong Solid Square Black (499 kr)
  3. Nike Revolution 7 (psv) Black/white-white (479 kr)
  4. Nike Revolution 6 Little Kids' Aura/multi-color-worn Blue (339 kr)
  5. Sunray Adjust 5 PS Little kids/GS Big kids Blue (259 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Nike Revolution 7 Baby/toddler Football Grey/midnight Navy-li (409 kr)
  2. Nike Revolution 6 Little Kids' Aura/multi-color-worn Blue (339 kr)
  3. Nike Revolution 7 (psv) Black/white-white (479 kr)
Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 32 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 17 queries ("skor", "pumma", "träningskläder"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
